name: Tallow Worker's Reinforced Coat
description: A heavy waxed canvas coat worn by Tallow Ring workers handling hot product — the multiple layered panels at the torso offer modest protection without advertising armor to anyone looking.

name: Waste-Runner's Padded Vest
description: A salvaged garment rebuilt from layered cloth, raw leather, and whatever rigid scrap could be stitched inside the panels — standard kit for anyone who goes beyond the gates and expects to come back.

name: Apothecary's Treated Duster
description: A long leather coat impregnated with alum, coal-tar oil, and one other compound its maker has not disclosed — worn by compound workers and black-market chemists who expect proximity to reactive substances.

name: Compact Watchman's Chainmail Vest
description: Issued to active Compact watchmen on district assignment — a riveted mail vest worn under a tabard with the ward colors, returned to the armory at end of service rotation or recovered from the body.

name: League Foreman's Half-Plate
description: A composite of brigandine chest-panels and spaulders issued to League floor-supervisors who need to walk the smelting halls during labor disputes — not elegant, but made by people who know what metal can do.

name: Harbor Ward Boarding Coat
description: Oiled canvas over a hidden layer of ring-mail, shaped for movement on wet dock planking — Harbor ward enforcers use these when they board vessels for compliance inspections that sometimes end badly.

name: Salvaged Pre-Breach Plate (Partial)
description: Chest, gorget, and pauldrons recovered from a sealed lower-district armory — the steel predates the Breach and is visibly superior to current production, though the matching leg-harness was not found.

name: League Armory Chest-Shell
description: A full breastplate produced by the League's private armorers for senior enforcement officers — the design borrows from pre-Breach technical drawings and is not commercially available, only issued or confiscated.

name: Ring Courier's Silent-Tread Leather
description: A precisely fitted leather harness made by a Ring-affiliated cobbler — the sole inlays muffle footfall on stone, the cut allows full arm rotation, and the fit is taken from measurements, not stock sizes.

name: Compact Assessor's Reinforced Coat
description: A formal-cut wool coat worn by Compact property assessors in contested districts — the inner panels are padded with layers of densely woven scrap-metal rings, invisible unless the wearer is searched.
